response: Government Polytechnic Chapra is a government technical institute located in Marhaura, Saran, Bihar. It is affiliated with SBTE Bihar and approved by AICTE.
Admission is done through DCECE entrance exam conducted by BCECE Board. After qualifying, students go through counselling and document verification.
The college offers diploma courses in Computer Science, Civil Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering and Automobile Engineering.
Each branch has a semester-wise syllabus including theory subjects, practical labs, assignments, and projects designed by SBTE Bihar.
Each department has dedicated labs such as Computer Lab, Electrical Lab, Mechanical Workshop, Civil Lab, and Electronics Lab depending on the branch.
The college has experienced faculty members and HODs in each department who guide students in both theory and practical learning.
Hostel facilities are available for both boys and girls depending on availability. Rooms are allotted based on merit and distance.
The hostel mess provides simple vegetarian and non-vegetarian meals including breakfast, lunch, and dinner with a fixed monthly menu.
Exams are conducted semester-wise by SBTE Bihar including internal assessment, practical exams, viva, and end-semester written exams.
Results are published on the official SBTE Bihar website. Students can check their SGPA and CGPA using their roll number.
The college provides placement assistance through training and placement cell, helping students get opportunities in companies and industries.
The college organizes sports events under UMANG where students participate in cricket, football, badminton, and athletics.
The college has a well-equipped library with academic books, reference materials, and journals for all branches.
The campus environment is disciplined, peaceful, and focused on technical learning with proper facilities and greenery.
The college is located in Marhaura, Saran district, Bihar. The nearest railway station is Chapra Junction.
Scholarships are available for SC/ST/OBC/Minority students through Bihar government schemes and merit-based programs.

patterns: how much does complete diploma cost
response: The fee structure of the college includes multiple components. Admission fee is approximately ₹1665. For each semester exam form fee is charged yearly. Enrollment fee is generally not charged separately. Hostel fee is around ₹4500 per semester. Exam form fees vary: for general category boys it is around ₹1500, for girls and SC/ST students it is around ₹1000. Overall, the total cost of the complete diploma course including hostel (excluding mess food) is approximately ₹10,000 to ₹12,000.

Students can report complaints related to academics, hostel, mess, labs, or any other issue through the college grievance system or directly to the concerned HOD or college authority. Serious issues like ragging should be reported immediately to the anti-ragging cell or college administration. Complaints are taken seriously and resolved as soon as possible.
The college is headed by a Principal who manages academic and administrative activities. For the latest principal details, students should check the official notice board or website.
The college has an Anti-Ragging Cell. Ragging is strictly banned and punishable. Students can report anonymously and strict action is taken.
Students need to fill exam forms online through SBTE Bihar portal before semester exams and submit required fees within the deadline.
The college follows SGPA and CGPA system. Marks include internal assessment, practical exams, and semester theory exams conducted by SBTE Bihar.
Practical exams include lab work and viva. Students must perform experiments and answer questions to pass practical subjects.
Students have to complete project work in final year which includes designing, implementation, and viva presentation based on their branch.
Students must follow strict discipline including attendance, uniform, respectful behavior, and academic honesty. Violations may lead to action.
The college organizes cultural events, technical activities, sports competitions, and student participation programs for overall development.
Lab classes are scheduled along with theory classes and may extend for 2 to 3 hours depending on subject requirements.

patterns: can i give exam without attendance
response: If attendance is below 75 percent, students may not be allowed to appear in exams unless special permission is granted.
The college has a large number of students across different branches and semesters, creating a diverse academic environment.
Students can check important notices on the college notice board or official website for updates regarding exams, holidays, and events.
The campus is generally safe with proper monitoring. Hostel and campus rules are strictly followed to ensure student safety. Cameras are installed everywhere.

Each branch has its own semester-wise syllabus designed by SBTE Bihar. It includes theory subjects, practical labs, and projects. Students can download the detailed syllabus from the official SBTE Bihar website.
Enrollment is done after admission through SBTE Bihar. Students receive a unique enrollment number which is used for exams and results.
Required documents include 10th marksheet, admit card, DCECE rank card, caste certificate (if applicable), income certificate, passport size photos, Aadhaar card, and residence certificate.
Each department has qualified faculty members including lecturers and HODs who guide students in both theory and practical learning.
Students are required to complete assignments, lab records, and practical files regularly. These are part of internal assessment marks.
The college has multiple labs including Computer Lab, Electrical Lab, Electronics Lab, Mechanical Workshop, Civil Lab, and Automobile Lab depending on branches.
Classrooms are spacious and suitable for learning. Some rooms may have smart teaching facilities like projectors.
The campus includes classrooms, labs, library, playground, hostel, canteen, and academic environment for technical learning.

Each department has a Head of Department (HOD). The HODs are: Dr. Rama Singh (Computer Science), Dr. Sunny Kumar (Civil), Prof. Mukesh Kumar (Mechanical), Prof. Manish Kumar (Electrical), Prof. Shushant Kumar (Automobile), and Prof. Om Prakash Aditya (Electronics). For updated names, students should check the official notice board or college website.
The college organizes seminars, webinars, and workshops to improve technical knowledge and industry exposure of students.
Students can check official information on the college website Visit Official Website⁠  and contact through the official email provided by the institution (email: gpchapra@gmail.com).
The Principal, Dr. Anil Kumar Singh, is the head of the institution responsible for managing academics and administration. For the latest details, refer to official sources.
Students can also participate in additional courses like KYP (Kushal Yuva Program), Cisco networking, and other skill development programs.
Basic medical and first aid facilities are available in the college for students in case of emergency.
Students can report complaints to HOD, faculty, or grievance cell. Serious issues are handled by administration.
The college is located in Marhaura. Students can travel from Chapra via auto or bus. The nearest railway station is Chapra Junction.
Internal exams are conducted by the college and marks are included in final result.
Practical exams include lab performance and viva questions conducted before / after final exams.
Students can download results from the official SBTE Bihar website using roll number.
The exam includes internal marks, practical marks, and final semester written exam conducted by SBTE Bihar.
If a student fails in any subjects more than five times, they may get backlog and need to clear it in next exams. Multiple failures may lead to year back.
Students who fail can appear in supplementary exams conducted by SBTE Bihar to clear their subjects.

patterns: can i go next year with back
response: Students may be promoted with limited backlogs (6), but too many backlogs can stop promotion. Rules are decided by SBTE Bihar.

patterns: can i take admission in 3rd year
response: Direct admission in 3rd year is not allowed. Only lateral entry in 2nd year is possible.

The nearest railway station to Government Polytechnic Chapra is Marhaura Railway Station, which is located a few kilometers from the college. Another major nearby station is Chapra Junction, which is around 20–25 km away and well connected to major cities. From these stations, students can easily reach the college by auto or local transport.

Government Polytechnic Chapra is a well-maintained and disciplined college with a peaceful environment. It provides good technical education, basic facilities like labs, library, and hostel, and supports students in learning and skill development.

Student life includes attending classes, practical labs, completing assignments, participating in events, and preparing for exams in a disciplined environment.
The college maintains discipline regarding attendance, uniform, and behavior to ensure a proper learning environment.
After diploma, students can go for B.Tech through lateral entry or apply for jobs depending on their skills.
You can ask about admission, fees, courses, hostel, placement, exams, or campus details of Government Polytechnic Chapra.

Our college follows a strict uniform policy. Girls wear a white suit with black salwar and dupatta, while boys wear a white shirt, black pants, a tie, and formal shoes. Only formal dress is allowed on campus. Students are also provided with a college ID card after admission. It is mandatory to carry the ID card every day inside the campus, and it should not be lost or misplaced.

Our college provides separate hostel facilities for boys and girls. Each room is equipped with basic amenities like an almirah, study table, bed, and charging points. A minimum of two students can stay in one room. Gym facilities are available in the boys' hostel, while currently they are not available in the girls' hostel.

The final result in our college is based on a combination of assignments, lab work, attendance, class participation, internal tests, viva, and external examinations. The external exam is usually of 70 marks, while the remaining 20–30 marks are internal assessment given by subject teachers. The external exam includes 10 objective questions (2 marks each), 5 short-answer questions (4 marks each), and 5 long-answer questions (5 or 6 marks each).

Our college provides excellent study facilities. We have a well-equipped library with a variety of books. Students are given a library card, which allows them to borrow books and study in the library. We also have computer and MATLAB labs where students can study using computers, watch educational videos, and access digital resources. Overall, both books and computer-based learning facilities are available for students.

The environment around our college is very convenient and student-friendly. There are many shops near the campus, including cyber cafes where students can easily complete tasks like form filling and other academic work. Tea stalls and other small shops are also available, so students can find all necessary supplementary items easily. Additionally, there is a Sudha Cafe inside the college campus for refreshments.

A diploma course is generally considered equivalent to the intermediate (10+2) level in terms of academic stage. It is an undergraduate-level program that focuses more on practical and technical skills. After completing a diploma, students can either apply for jobs or continue their education through higher studies such as B.Tech or B.E. via lateral entry.

Diploma or polytechnic courses offer many benefits. They focus on practical and technical skills, making students job-ready in a shorter time. The course duration is usually less than a degree, so students can start earning earlier. It is also more affordable compared to degree programs. After completing a diploma, students can either get a job or continue higher studies like B.Tech through lateral entry. These courses are especially useful for building strong technical knowledge and skills.

Our college has many professors who are highly knowledgeable in their respective subjects and very friendly in nature. Each branch has three or more teachers who specifically teach and support their department students. In addition, we have specialized teachers such as sports teachers, English teachers, yoga instructors, and many more to ensure overall development of students.

The college follows a fixed daily schedule from 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM. There is a lunch break from 1:00 PM to 2:00 PM. Classes are conducted throughout the day with proper breaks, and the schedule may slightly vary depending on the department and semester.

Required documents for admission include 10th marksheet, admit card, Aadhaar card, and at least 10 passport-size photos. Students also need to submit caste certificate (if applicable), income certificate, domicile certificate, and migration certificate if required. It is recommended to carry 2–3 photocopies of each document.
